title: Tapping to Feel Gratitude for Present Wealth
subtitle: A tapping practice for pausing the rush toward the next goal and landing fully in the abundance already here
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 78 of a 12-week money programme. The script works with the habit of rushing past what is already built toward the next target, and moves toward a full, present-tense experience of the income, assets, and freedom already in place.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 78 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you keep moving the goalpost on what counts as 'enough' and want to stop and let the current wealth land.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 78 of a 12-week money programme. The first day of Week 12: Live Abundance as My Normal. The script works with the habit of focusing on the next goal before the current one has been felt, and moves toward a grounded, present-tense experience of the wealth and freedom already here.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I sometimes rush toward the next goal, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I forget to pause and feel grateful, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I focus on what's next more than what's here, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I stop and notice how far I've come.
Side of eye: I see the income and assets already here.
Under eye: I feel thankful for every penny and property.
Under nose: I let gratitude fill this moment.
Chin: I savour the freedom I already live in.
Collarbone: I'm proud of the wealth I've built.
Under arm: I honour today's abundance.
Top of head: I feel rich right now.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I live in daily gratitude for wealth.
Side of eye: I thank money for supporting my life.
Under eye: I trust that appreciation draws in more.
Under nose: I celebrate all that already flows to me.
Chin: I keep my heart open and grateful.
Collarbone: I let contentment and expansion grow together.
Under arm: I enjoy every bit of abundance in this moment.
Top of head: I am grateful and fulfilled right now.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 78 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
